title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Contract Clinic: Goal First
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt goal first increment in a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Goal First
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Goal First contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.

content.text: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Goal First, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A prompt should begin with every action the model must take. This fails the Goal First contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Goal First evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-goal-first-gmh4lp" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Goal First into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-goal-first-gmh4lp
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Goal First model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal First applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
